steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says Burns received the fellowship ‘because of his lifetime contributions to the arts.’ The blank describes what happened to Burns — he was picked to receive the honor based on merit.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘confused’: means disoriented or baffled. Someone receiving an honor due to merit is not described as confused. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘reluctant’: means unwilling. The passage gives no hint Burns was unwilling; the signal is that he earned the fellowship. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘ineligible’: means not qualified. This directly contradicts the passage — Burns received the fellowship, so he was clearly eligible. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘chosen’: means selected. Substituting: Burns was chosen for the fellowship because of his lifetime contributions. That matches the signal perfectly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
